commands.review_summary:
The PR switches the stable gateway package to a lockfile-backed npm shrinkwrap build, keeps the source/pnpm builder as fallback, adds npm wrapper locks for OpenClaw and ACPX, stages runtime contents, updates package checks, and adjusts pin/Garnix automation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a packaging cleanup PR, not a bug report. Review focused on current source behavior, the PR head diff, package-contract checks, and pin automation paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer review of the default package-source flip and latest-head CI/package-contract evidence, not an automated repair.

target.title: Default gateway to npm shrinkwrap
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## Human Intent

> clean elegant install in nix of npm package; we have many hacks from dirty packaging, we want to clean them up as much as possible to reduce total build time (both in CI and locally) and make packaging less fragile and more predictable; using tools like shrinkwrap if we can; and other optimizations as necessary. fewer dependencies in the tree is also good, faster build time is good, smaller is good, etc, you know the drill.

## What

- Flips the stable `openclaw-gateway` package to build from the published `openclaw` npm package by default when `gatewayNpmDepsHash` is present.
- Keeps the old source/pnpm builder as the explicit fallback for `gatewaySrc` and dogfood-style source pins.
- Adds lockfile-backed npm wrapper builds for `openclaw` and `@openclaw/acpx`, with version assertions against `sourceInfo.releaseVersion`.
- Stages the npm package into the runtime layout nix-openclaw already checks: top-level extension manifests, `dist-runtime/extensions`, bundled ACPX compatibility files, and the wrapped `openclaw` binary.
- Moves Nix-store plugin root trust into an npm-dist patch script and adds package-content assertions so the default package must keep loading Nix-managed runtime plugins.
- Teaches `scripts/update-pins.sh` to refresh wrapper locks and npm dependency hashes while still validating the source fallback pnpm hash.

## Why This Shape

The upstream `v2026.6.1` release publishes a root `npm-shrinkwrap.json`. npm documents shrinkwrap as a publishable lockfile and says it takes precedence over `package-lock.json` for published package dependency trees. Nixpkgs' current JavaScript packaging docs recommend respecting the upstream package manager/lockfile and document `buildNpmPackage` with `npmDepsHash` as the fixed-output npm cache path; `importNpmLock` is promising, but this PR uses `buildNpmPackage` because it handled the published package path directly and predictably in the current tree.

Docs checked:

- https://nixos.org/manual/nixpkgs/stable/#javascript
- https://docs.npmjs.com/files/package-lock.json/
- https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/commands/npm-shrinkwrap

## Measured Impact

Compared against the previous stable source-builder output:

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `openclaw-gateway` closure | 2,273,877,888 bytes | 915,457,000 bytes | -1,358,420,888 bytes, 59.7% smaller |
| `openclaw` closure | 3,215,431,032 bytes | 1,857,010,136 bytes | -1,358,420,896 bytes, 42.2% smaller |
| gateway output size | 2.1G | 53M | materially smaller |
| `lib/openclaw/node_modules` package manifests | 1,217 | 356 | -861, 70.7% fewer |
| files under `lib/openclaw` | 105,509 | 36,363 | -69,146, 65.5% fewer |

Build-time proof:

- New default gateway forced rebuild succeeded in `real 51.03` seconds:
  `nix build --accept-flake-config --rebuild --no-link --print-out-paths .#openclaw-gateway`
- Old source-builder forced rebuild ran for `real 399.37` seconds and then failed Nix's determinism check because the rebuilt output differed:
  `nix build --rebuild --no-link --print-out-paths '/nix/store/w7z8m445bb9ykm4fzxvdmjvv8cnyjnq0-openclaw-gateway-unstable-2e08f0f4.drv^*'`

## Proof

Final aggregate on the committed tree:

- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-out-paths .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`
  - `/nix/store/pmxsi1k46aymknfaxnvvli99j0hcbw3v-nix-openclaw-ci`
  - `/nix/store/bkymrn690awpl4myqwzmhzidqa84ajr9-nix-openclaw-ci`

Additional checks run:

- `GITHUB_ACTIONS=true scripts/update-pins.sh select` -> no update selected; source/app remain `v2026.6.1`.
- `scripts/check-flake-lock-owners.sh`
- `bash -n scripts/update-pins.sh`
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs`
- `node --check nix/scripts/patch-openclaw-npm-dist.mjs`
- `nix flake show --accept-flake-config --json`
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check`

## Notes / Follow-up

This PR preserves the user-facing package contract: users still install `openclaw`; `openclaw-gateway` remains a component output for modules/checks/debugging.

The runtime-plugin pipeline is intentionally not collapsed in this PR. The result here is evidence that the same lockfile/shrinkwrap direction is viable for default gateway packaging and ACPX. A follow-up can generalize the runtime plugin packaging/staging further where upstream plugin packages expose complete shrinkwrap/package roots, but this PR keeps that change bounded so the default package flip is reviewable.
target.author: joshp123
